title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear a man giving information to young people at a summer sports camp.
audio_script: Man: Welcome to the first day of the summer sports camp. My name is Ben, and I'll be helping you during the week. Before we go outside, I want to explain the programme. Today we will start with tennis, but tomorrow's main sport is [14]basketball[/14]. That will be in the sports hall, so it will happen even if the weather is bad. Each morning begins at [15]nine[/15] o'clock, but the gates open at eight thirty if your parents need to drop you off early. You should all have trainers and a water bottle. Please write your name on your bottle because many of them look the same. On Wednesday we are going swimming, so you must bring a [16]towel[/16]. You do not need to bring money for lunch. Everyone will eat in the [17]canteen[/17], and there will be sandwiches, pasta, and fruit. In the afternoon, you can choose one activity. This week the new choice is [18]climbing[/18], which is on the small wall behind the gym. Helmets will be provided. Finally, parents should collect you from the green gate, not the car park. If there is a problem, please phone the camp office before [19]five[/19] o'clock. There will be short breaks between activities, and you can refill your water bottles at the taps near the changing rooms. If you feel tired or unwell at any point, tell one of the coaches immediately. We want you to enjoy the camp, but safety comes first. Please check the notice board before you leave, because small changes to rooms or times may be written there. If you are not sure about anything, ask a member of staff.

questions.explanation: The speaker says tomorrow's main sport is basketball.
The speaker says each morning begins at nine o'clock.
The speaker says students must bring a towel for swimming.
The speaker says everyone will eat in the canteen.
The speaker says the new choice is climbing.
The speaker says parents should phone before five o'clock.
